mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates expanding labor demand and rising aggregate income, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the share of positive 20d returns in months with payroll gains above 150k is not significantly higher than 50% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market that lifts wage growth and consumption, supporting equity valuations, but only when the level falls below a threshold indicative of genuine strength rather than labor force shrinkage.
failure_condition: In trigger windows, the median 20d forward return is negative or not statistically different from nontrigger windows (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) coupled with a low but increasing federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals that the Federal Reserve is behind the curve, forcing future tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20d forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the cross-sectional rank IC of the joint signal is below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of residential investment and consumer confidence. During the post-2008 recovery phase, rising starts reflect improving demand and credit conditions, which positively feed into broader economic activity and equity returns.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery (GDP growth below trend, unemployment > 6%)
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward return correlation with HOUST 3-month change is negative or below 0.10 over any 2-year period, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy in terms of Sharpe ratio by at least 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate profits and investor sentiment over short horizons of about one week.
regime_hint: expansionary phase (INDPRO level above prior 12-month average)
failure_condition: In trigger weeks, the 5-day forward return has a negative median, or the average return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising advance retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which constitutes the largest component of GDP and directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change versus forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling period, or the long-short portfolio based on RSAFS deciles yields negative cumulative return over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with very low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) creates an environment of robust economic expansion with accommodative monetary policy, which is exceptionally positive for equity valuations and forward returns over a quarter.
regime_hint: goldilocks expansion (above-trend growth, low inflation, easy monetary policy)
failure_condition: In triggered quarters, 60-day forward returns are negative or not materially above the unconditional median (one-sample Wilcoxon signed-rank p > 0.10), or the joint signal's hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, a moderate rise in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) is interpreted as evidence of healing demand rather than overheating, which supports corporate pricing power and equity valuations, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: In months where CPI YoY is below 3%, the correlation between CPI change and forward 20d returns is negative, or the average return following rising CPI months is less than the average following falling CPI months (paired difference t-test p > 0.05).
